facts.text: Egypt sits in northeastern Africa and connects Africa with the Middle East through the Sinai Peninsula.
Cairo is Egypt's capital and one of the largest cities in the Arab world and Africa.
The Nile River runs through Egypt and has shaped settlement, farming, and transportation for thousands of years.
The Pyramids of Giza and the Great Sphinx are among the most famous landmarks on Earth.
Egypt's landscape is dominated by desert, but the Nile Valley and Delta are fertile and densely populated.
The Suez Canal, opened in 1869, links the Mediterranean Sea with the Red Sea and is one of the world's most important shipping routes.
Arabic is the official language of Egypt.
Egyptian cuisine includes dishes such as koshari, ful medames, ta'ameya, and grilled meats.
Alexandria on the Mediterranean coast has been a major port and cultural center since ancient times.
Tourism is a major part of Egypt's economy thanks to its history, beaches, diving sites, and archaeological treasures.
Egypt hosted one of the earliest great civilizations, but it is also a modern nation with massive cities, universities, and industries.
The country has influenced art, architecture, religion, archaeology, and popular imagination around the globe.
Because of its geography, Egypt has long been strategically important in trade, empire, and regional politics.
The Aswan High Dam, completed in the 20th century, significantly changed the flow and management of the Nile.
The Pyramids of Giza are among the most famous ancient monuments in the world.
They remain central to the image of Egypt and to the study of ancient civilization.
Pyramids of Giza is one of the most recognizable places or topics associated with Egypt.
Travel guides about Egypt frequently feature Pyramids of Giza.
Learning about Pyramids of Giza helps explain part of the geography, culture, or history of Egypt.
For many visitors, Pyramids of Giza stands out as a major highlight of Egypt.
Maps, documentaries, and guidebooks about Egypt often highlight Pyramids of Giza.
The story of Pyramids of Giza is closely tied to how many people understand Egypt.
People around the world often associate Egypt with places or traditions like Pyramids of Giza.
Pyramids of Giza is frequently mentioned when describing the diversity and appeal of Egypt.
